renderedFields.description:
Problem (user-facing)

Studio surfaces internal, code-like validation text in several module flows. Messages expose raw error codes (e.g. [LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE]) and long concatenated strings instead of clear, human-readable copy that states which tool / which field is wrong and what to change.

A) Publish Module Release

In Publish Module Release (e.g. agents-dev.kore.ai), failed POST …/releases validation is shown in the modal as a single red block of text similar to:

[LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE] tool:list_users: HTTP tool "list_users" has a non-templated auth value. Use auth_profile_ref or {{env. / config. templating instead.. [LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE] tool:get_user: …}}

Issues for users

  • Bracketed codes read like engineer-only diagnostics, not product copy.
  • Repeating the same rule twice (per tool) is noisy; better as one summary + per-tool bullets.
  • Which setting to open (Tool Library → which tool → auth / auth profile) is not spelled out.
  • Field-level mapping (e.g. "Auth on HTTP tool list_users") should be visually primary; machine code can be secondary or in a "Details" expando.

B) Deployment — module dependency resolution

When module dependency resolution fails during deployment, the UI can show a generic toast, e.g. Module dependency resolution failed for this deployment. Fix the reported issues and retry. — without saying whether the blocker is auth profile, module version, entry agent, etc.

Desired outcome / acceptance criteria

  1. Primary message in plain language — e.g. "Publishing blocked: HTTP tools must not embed raw auth secrets." Then per affected tool with field context (tool name, "Authentication" / auth_profile_ref).
  2. Structured errors for UI — API returns items with toolId or toolName, field / path, userMessage, optional code for support logs — not one concatenated string.
  3. Actionable next steps — Link or CTA to Tool Library / Auth profiles as appropriate; no stack traces or secret values.
  4. Consistency — Publish modal, deployment toasts, and any diagnostics[] / moduleBuild surfaces use the same vocabulary and formatting rules.
  5. Deploy flow — Same bar: name the missing or invalid prerequisite (e.g. auth profile name), not only MODULE_BUILD_FAILED.

Background — deployment repro (unchanged)

Published module imports into another project; deployment fails with MODULE_BUILD_FAILED, diagnostics such as AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND (source: auth-profile), mountedAgentCount / mountedToolCount 0. Backend may already expose richer diagnostics than the toast; UI must surface them clearly.

Steps: publish module → import in second project → create deployment → {{POST /api/projects/

{projectId}

/deployments}}.

Hypothesis (backend, separate from copy): module still references auth profiles from source context; import may not remap — track or split to a linked issue as needed.

Related: ABLP-1009 (Create Deployment UI {{

{status}

}}). Host (sanitized): agents-dev.kore.ai — do not paste tokens in Jira.

Impact

High — users cannot self-serve from current strings; module publish and cross-project deploy both feel broken.

items.fromString:
h2. Problem (user-facing)

When *module dependency resolution* fails during *deployment*, the Studio UI surfaces a *generic, low-signal message* that does not explain *what failed*, *which prerequisite is missing*, or *which field / entity the user should fix*.

Example toast:

{quote}_Module dependency resolution failed for this deployment. Fix the reported issues and retry._{quote}

Users cannot tell from the toast alone whether the issue is an *auth profile*, *module version*, *entry agent*, or something else. *Ideally the UI should clearly name the offending field or domain object* (for example: missing auth profile {{reqres_key_saiteja}}, unresolved module release, invalid {{agentVersionManifest}} entry, etc.) and point to the next corrective action.

h2. Desired outcome / acceptance criteria

# *Field- or object-level clarity* — Surface *which* dependency or configuration item is wrong (e.g. auth profile name, module alias, agent name in manifest), not only a high-level code.
# *Actionable copy* — Short explanation of *what to do next* (create auth profile in this project, re-import module, adjust manifest), without leaking secrets or internal stack traces.
# *Consistency* — Align toast, inline form errors (if any), and *deployment / moduleBuild diagnostics* so the same structured detail is visible in the UI where possible.
# *API contract* — Prefer reusing or extending structured {{diagnostics[]}} (e.g. {{source}}, {{code}}, *target field / resource id*) so the UI can render specific messages without guessing.

----

h2. Background (technical repro — unchanged)

A *published module* (e.g. 1 agent, 2 tools) *imports successfully* into another project. *Deployment* then fails with {{MODULE_BUILD_FAILED}} and diagnostics such as {{AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND}} ({{source}}: *auth-profile*). {{moduleBuild}} shows {{mountedAgentCount: 0}}, {{mountedToolCount: 0}}.

The backend may expose richer diagnostics than the toast; this ticket tracks *closing that gap in the UI* (and tightening messages end-to-end if needed).

h3. Steps to reproduce

# Publish a module from a source project (≥1 agent, tools).
# In a *second project*, import the module — confirm success.
# *Deployments* → create deployment (e.g. dev, entry agent from module).
# Submit → {{POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deployments}}.

h3. Observed backend shape (for implementers)

* {{error.code}}: *MODULE_BUILD_FAILED*
* {{moduleBuild.success}}: *false*, mounted counts *0*
* {{diagnostics[]}}: e.g. *AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND* from *auth-profile*

h3. Hypothesis (root cause of failure — separate from UX scope)

Module may still reference auth profiles from *source* context; import may not remap/clone or enforce prerequisites. Track under this or a linked backend issue as appropriate.

h3. Related

* Create Deployment UI may show unresolved {{{status}}} — *ABLP-1009*.
* *Cross-project module deploy* blocked until prerequisites + *clear UI errors* are addressed.

h3. Request context (sanitized)

* *Host:* agents-dev.kore.ai
* *Endpoint:* {{POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deployments}}
* Do not paste bearer tokens in Jira.

h3. Impact

*High* — users cannot self-serve from the current message; module reuse feels broken even when the underlying fix may be a single missing auth profile.
items.toString:
h2. Problem (user-facing)

Studio surfaces *internal, code-like validation text* in several *module* flows. Messages expose raw *error codes* (e.g. {{[LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE]}}) and long concatenated strings instead of *clear, human-readable copy* that states *which tool / which field* is wrong and *what to change*.

h3. A) Publish Module Release

In *Publish Module Release* (e.g. agents-dev.kore.ai), failed {{POST …/releases}} validation is shown in the modal as a single red block of text similar to:

{quote}{{[LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE] tool:list_users: HTTP tool "list_users" has a non-templated auth value. Use auth_profile_ref or {{env.*}} / {{config.*}} templating instead.. [LITERAL_AUTH_VALUE] tool:get_user: …}}{quote}

*Issues for users*

* Bracketed codes read like *engineer-only* diagnostics, not product copy.
* Repeating the same rule twice (per tool) is noisy; better as *one summary + per-tool bullets*.
* *Which setting to open* (Tool Library → which tool → auth / auth profile) is not spelled out.
* *Field-level* mapping (e.g. "Auth on HTTP tool {{list_users}}") should be visually primary; machine {{code}} can be secondary or in a "Details" expando.

h3. B) Deployment — module dependency resolution

When *module dependency resolution* fails during *deployment*, the UI can show a *generic* toast, e.g. _Module dependency resolution failed for this deployment. Fix the reported issues and retry._ — without saying whether the blocker is *auth profile*, *module version*, *entry agent*, etc.

----

h2. Desired outcome / acceptance criteria

# *Primary message in plain language* — e.g. "Publishing blocked: HTTP tools must not embed raw auth secrets." Then *per affected tool* with *field* context (tool name, "Authentication" / {{auth_profile_ref}}).
# *Structured errors for UI* — API returns items with {{toolId}} or {{toolName}}, {{field}} / {{path}}, {{userMessage}}, optional {{code}} for support logs — not one concatenated string.
# *Actionable next steps* — Link or CTA to *Tool Library* / *Auth profiles* as appropriate; no stack traces or secret values.
# *Consistency* — Publish modal, deployment toasts, and any {{diagnostics[]}} / {{moduleBuild}} surfaces use the same vocabulary and formatting rules.
# *Deploy flow* — Same bar: name the *missing or invalid prerequisite* (e.g. auth profile name), not only {{MODULE_BUILD_FAILED}}.

----

h2. Background — deployment repro (unchanged)

Published module imports into another project; *deployment* fails with {{MODULE_BUILD_FAILED}}, diagnostics such as {{AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND}} ({{source}}: auth-profile), {{mountedAgentCount}} / {{mountedToolCount}} *0*. Backend may already expose richer diagnostics than the toast; *UI must surface them clearly*.

*Steps:* publish module → import in second project → create deployment → {{POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deployments}}.

*Hypothesis (backend, separate from copy):* module still references auth profiles from source context; import may not remap — track or split to a linked issue as needed.

*Related:* *ABLP-1009* (Create Deployment UI {{{status}}}). *Host (sanitized):* agents-dev.kore.ai — do not paste tokens in Jira.

h3. Impact

*High* — users cannot self-serve from current strings; module publish and cross-project deploy both feel broken.

items.fromString: Deployment UI: module dependency resolution errors are vague — show which field/prerequisite failed (e.g. auth profile)
items.toString: Studio: module publish & deploy show code-like errors — need plain language + which tool/field/prerequisite failed

items.fromString:
h2. Summary

A *published module* (e.g. *1 agent*, *2 tools*, version such as *v1.0.2* / *v102*) *imports successfully* into a *different project*. *Deployment* then fails with {{MODULE_BUILD_FAILED}} and diagnostics including {{AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND}} (source: *auth-profile*). {{moduleBuild}} shows {{mountedAgentCount: 0}}, {{mountedToolCount: 0}}.

Toast: _Module dependency resolution failed for this deployment. Fix the reported issues and retry._

h2. Steps to reproduce

# Publish a module from a source project (≥1 agent, tools).
# In a *second project*, *Import module* — confirm success.
# *Deployments* → create deployment (dev env, entry agent from module e.g. {{Module_executor}}, version manifest as in UI).
# Submit → {{POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deployments}}.

h2. Expected

Deploy resolves dependencies (including *auth profiles*) in the *target project*, or blocks with clear prerequisites. Agents/tools mount.

h2. Actual

* {{error.code}}: *MODULE_BUILD_FAILED*
* {{moduleBuild.success}}: *false*, *mountedAgentCount* / *mountedToolCount*: *0*
* {{diagnostics[]}}: *AUTH_PROFILE_NOT_FOUND* (or *AUTH_PROFILE_**) from *auth-profile*

h2. Request context (sanitized)

* *Host:* agents-dev.kore.ai
* *Endpoint:* {{POST /api/projects/{projectId}/deployments}}
* *Example projectId:* {{019e1709-ae31-7734-9778-9b92f5ce5205}}
* *Body shape:* {{environment}}, {{entryAgentName}}, {{label}}, {{description}}, {{agentVersionManifest}} (agent → semver).

Do not paste bearer tokens in Jira.

h2. Hypothesis

Module still references auth profiles from *source* context; import does not remap/clone or enforce prerequisites.

h2. Related

Create Deployment may show unresolved {{{status}}} — *ABLP-1009*.

h2. Impact

High — cross-project reuse of published modules blocked at deploy.
